questions.q: What is the main characteristic of share capital?
opts: (A) It is a type of loan capital
(B) It is a component of proprietorship capital
(C) It is a type of long-term debt
(D) It is a component of long-term capital
questions.explanation: Share capital is a type of long-term capital as it is raised through the issue of shares by the company.

questions.q: What is the difference between authorized capital and issued capital?
opts: (A) Authorized capital is the maximum amount that a company can borrow
(B) Issued capital is the maximum amount that a company can borrow
(C) Authorized capital is the maximum amount of share capital that a company can issue and issued capital is the amount of share capital actually issued
(D) Authorized capital is the amount of share capital actually issued and issued capital is the maximum amount that a company can issue
questions.explanation: Authorized capital is the maximum amount of share capital that a company can issue and issued capital is the amount of share capital actually issued.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a share premium account?
opts: (A) To record the excess of issue price over face value of shares
(B) To record the excess of face value over issue price of shares
(C) To record the dividend paid on shares
(D) To record the share capital subscribed by the public
questions.explanation: A share premium account is used to record the excess of issue price over face value of shares.

questions.q: What is the difference between equity shares and preference shares?
opts: (A) Equity shares have a fixed dividend and preference shares have a variable dividend
(B) Equity shares have a variable dividend and preference shares have a fixed dividend
(C) Equity shares have no voting rights and preference shares have voting rights
(D) Equity shares have voting rights and preference shares have no voting rights
questions.explanation: Equity shares have voting rights and preference shares have no voting rights.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a redemption fund?
opts: (A) To provide for the redemption of preference shares
(B) To provide for the redemption of equity shares
(C) To provide for the payment of dividend on preference shares
(D) To provide for the payment of dividend on equity shares
questions.explanation: A redemption fund is used to provide for the redemption of preference shares.

questions.q: What is the difference between a debenture and a debenture stock?
opts: (A) A debenture is a type of long-term debt and a debenture stock is a type of short-term debt
(B) A debenture is a type of short-term debt and a debenture stock is a type of long-term debt
(C) A debenture is a type of fixed interest-bearing debt and a debenture stock is a type of variable interest-bearing debt
(D) A debenture is a type of variable interest-bearing debt and a debenture stock is a type of fixed interest-bearing debt
questions.explanation: A debenture is a type of fixed interest-bearing debt and a debenture stock is a type of variable interest-bearing debt.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a debenture trustee?
opts: (A) To act as a representative of the debenture holders
(B) To act as a representative of the company
(C) To act as a representative of the bank
(D) To act as a representative of the government
questions.explanation: A debenture trustee acts as a representative of the debenture holders.

questions.q: What is the difference between a secured debenture and an unsecured debenture?
opts: (A) A secured debenture has a fixed interest rate and an unsecured debenture has a variable interest rate
(B) A secured debenture has a variable interest rate and an unsecured debenture has a fixed interest rate
(C) A secured debenture is backed by a mortgage or charge and an unsecured debenture is not backed by a mortgage or charge
(D) A secured debenture is not backed by a mortgage or charge and an unsecured debenture is backed by a mortgage or charge
questions.explanation: A secured debenture is backed by a mortgage or charge and an unsecured debenture is not backed by a mortgage or charge.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a mortgage debenture?
opts: (A) To provide a loan to the company
(B) To provide a guarantee to the lender
(C) To provide security to the lender by creating a charge on the assets of the company
(D) To provide a guarantee to the company
questions.explanation: A mortgage debenture provides security to the lender by creating a charge on the assets of the company.

questions.q: What is the difference between a convertible debenture and a non-convertible debenture?
opts: (A) A convertible debenture can be converted into equity shares and a non-convertible debenture cannot be converted into equity shares
(B) A convertible debenture cannot be converted into equity shares and a non-convertible debenture can be converted into equity shares
(C) A convertible debenture has a fixed interest rate and a non-convertible debenture has a variable interest rate
(D) A convertible debenture has a variable interest rate and a non-convertible debenture has a fixed interest rate
questions.explanation: A convertible debenture can be converted into equity shares and a non-convertible debenture cannot be converted into equity shares.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a sinking fund?
opts: (A) To provide for the redemption of debentures
(B) To provide for the payment of dividend on debentures
(C) To provide for the payment of interest on debentures
(D) To provide for the payment of premium on debentures
questions.explanation: A sinking fund is used to provide for the redemption of debentures.

questions.q: What is the difference between a registered debenture stock and an unregistered debenture stock?
opts: (A) A registered debenture stock is issued in a dematerialized form and an unregistered debenture stock is issued in a physical form
(B) A registered debenture stock is issued in a physical form and an unregistered debenture stock is issued in a dematerialized form
(C) A registered debenture stock has a fixed interest rate and an unregistered debenture stock has a variable interest rate
(D) A registered debenture stock has a variable interest rate and an unregistered debenture stock has a fixed interest rate
questions.explanation: A registered debenture stock is issued in a dematerialized form and an unregistered debenture stock is issued in a physical form.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a debenture redemption reserve?
opts: (A) To provide for the redemption of debentures
(B) To provide for the payment of dividend on debentures
(C) To provide for the payment of interest on debentures
(D) To provide for the payment of premium on debentures
questions.explanation: A debenture redemption reserve is used to provide for the redemption of debentures.

questions.q: What is the difference between a secured loan and an unsecured loan?
opts: (A) A secured loan has a fixed interest rate and an unsecured loan has a variable interest rate
(B) A secured loan has a variable interest rate and an unsecured loan has a fixed interest rate
(C) A secured loan is backed by a mortgage or charge and an unsecured loan is not backed by a mortgage or charge
(D) A secured loan is not backed by a mortgage or charge and an unsecured loan is backed by a mortgage or charge
questions.explanation: A secured loan is backed by a mortgage or charge and an unsecured loan is not backed by a mortgage or charge.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a loan redemption fund?
opts: (A) To provide for the redemption of loans
(B) To provide for the payment of interest on loans
(C) To provide for the payment of premium on loans
(D) To provide for the payment of dividend on loans
questions.explanation: A loan redemption fund is used to provide for the redemption of loans.

questions.q: What is the difference between a registered loan and an unregistered loan?
opts: (A) A registered loan is issued in a dematerialized form and an unregistered loan is issued in a physical form
(B) A registered loan is issued in a physical form and an unregistered loan is issued in a dematerialized form
(C) A registered loan has a fixed interest rate and an unregistered loan has a variable interest rate
(D) A registered loan has a variable interest rate and an unregistered loan has a fixed interest rate
questions.explanation: A registered loan is issued in a dematerialized form and an unregistered loan is issued in a physical form.
